evals.prompt: I want to add dark mode to our Flutter app. The app uses Riverpod for state management and we already have a ThemeData defined in lib/core/theme/app_theme.dart. It should support system preference detection and manual toggle. Priority is medium.
evals.expected_output: A goal file written to Docs/Goals/dark-mode/add-dark-mode.md (or similar) with [Dark Mode] prefix in title, frontmatter (status: pending, priority: medium, created date), clear objective, context referencing the existing theme file, 3-7 verifiable acceptance criteria covering dark mode toggle and system preference, constraints section, and notes. Master.md is created or updated.
expectations: A file was written inside a feature subfolder under Docs/Goals/ (e.g., Docs/Goals/dark-mode/)
The filename is kebab-case derived from the task portion of the title (not the feature prefix)
The goal title uses the [Feature] Task format (e.g., # [Dark Mode] Add Dark Mode)
The file contains YAML frontmatter with status: pending
The file contains YAML frontmatter with priority: medium
The file contains YAML frontmatter with a created date in YYYY-MM-DD format
The file contains an '## Objective' section
The file contains an '## Acceptance Criteria' section with at least 3 criteria
Each acceptance criterion is a checkbox item (- [ ])
The file contains an '## Constraints' section
The acceptance criteria mention system preference detection
The acceptance criteria mention manual toggle
The agent verified the file exists after writing (read-back or confirmation)
Docs/Goals/Master.md was created or updated with the new goal row
Master.md contains a summary table with status counts
Master.md contains a row for the dark mode goal with status 'pending' and priority 'medium'

evals.prompt: new goal: add a leaderboard to our Unity game. It's a mobile casual game using Firebase. We need both daily and all-time leaderboards. High priority since soft launch is next month.
evals.expected_output: A goal file written to Docs/Goals/leaderboard/add-leaderboard.md with [Leaderboard] prefix, high priority, Firebase-aware context, acceptance criteria covering daily and all-time leaderboards, mobile constraints. Master.md is created or updated.
expectations: A file was written inside a feature subfolder under Docs/Goals/ (e.g., Docs/Goals/leaderboard/)
The goal title uses the [Feature] Task format (e.g., # [Leaderboard] Add Daily and All-Time Leaderboards)
The file contains YAML frontmatter with status: pending
The file contains YAML frontmatter with priority: high
The file contains an '## Acceptance Criteria' section with at least 3 criteria
The acceptance criteria mention both daily and all-time leaderboards
The context or constraints mention Firebase
The context or constraints mention mobile platform
The agent wrote the file before asking for final confirmation (write-first pattern)
The agent verified the file exists after writing
Docs/Goals/Master.md was created or updated with the new goal row
Master.md contains a row for the leaderboard goal with priority 'high'

evals.prompt: I need a goal for setting up CI/CD. We use GitHub Actions. The project is a Next.js app deployed on Vercel. I want automated testing on PR, preview deployments, and production deploy on merge to main.
evals.expected_output: A goal file written to Docs/Goals/ci-cd/set-up-ci-cd-pipeline.md with [CI/CD] prefix, covering CI/CD setup with GitHub Actions, Vercel, automated testing, preview deploys, and production deploy. Master.md is created or updated.
expectations: A file was written inside a feature subfolder under Docs/Goals/ (e.g., Docs/Goals/ci-cd/)
The goal title uses the [Feature] Task format (e.g., # [CI/CD] Set Up CI/CD Pipeline)
The filename is kebab-case of the task part only
The file contains YAML frontmatter with status: pending and a priority field
The file contains an '## Objective' section describing CI/CD setup
The file contains an '## Acceptance Criteria' section with at least 3 criteria
The acceptance criteria mention automated testing on PR
The acceptance criteria mention preview deployments
The acceptance criteria mention production deploy on merge to main
The context mentions GitHub Actions and Vercel
The file was written to disk (not just presented in conversation)
The agent verified the file exists after writing
Docs/Goals/Master.md was created or updated with the new goal row
Master.md contains a row for the CI/CD goal

evals.prompt: create a goal for adding user authentication with email/password and Google OAuth to our app
evals.expected_output: A goal file written to Docs/Goals/authentication/add-user-authentication.md with [Authentication] prefix. The skill should ask clarifying questions but MUST still produce a file on disk. Master.md is created or updated.
expectations: A file was written inside a feature subfolder under Docs/Goals/ (e.g., Docs/Goals/authentication/)
The goal title uses the [Feature] Task format (e.g., # [Authentication] Add Email and OAuth Login)
The skill asked at least one clarifying question before writing
The file contains YAML frontmatter with status: pending
The file contains an '## Acceptance Criteria' section with at least 3 criteria
The acceptance criteria cover email/password authentication
The acceptance criteria cover Google OAuth
The file was physically written to Docs/Goals/{feature-folder}/ (not just shown in chat)
The agent verified the file exists after writing
Docs/Goals/Master.md was created or updated with the new goal row

evals.prompt: I want to build a real-time chat feature AND also add push notifications AND also set up analytics tracking. Can you create goals for these?
evals.expected_output: The skill should recognize this as multiple goals and decompose them into separate files in feature-specific folders. Each goal gets its own file with [Feature] Task title format. Master.md is updated once with all new goals.
expectations: The skill identified this as multiple independent objectives
At least 2 separate goal files were written to Docs/Goals/
Each goal file is in its own feature subfolder (e.g., chat/, notifications/, analytics/)
Each goal title uses the [Feature] Task format
Each goal file has its own YAML frontmatter with status: pending
Each goal file has its own '## Acceptance Criteria' section
depends_on fields use relative paths from Docs/Goals/ where appropriate
All goal files were verified to exist on disk after writing
No goal was left unwritten (only discussed in conversation without file export)
Docs/Goals/Master.md was created or updated with rows for ALL new goals
Master.md summary counts reflect the total number of goals created
Master.md contains one row per created goal (not a single combined row)

evals.prompt: I want to update the existing goal in Docs/Goals/authentication/add-jwt-authentication.md — we decided to use RSA keys instead of HMAC for signing, and we need to add a criterion about automatic key rotation every 90 days. Also bump the priority to critical since it's blocking the B2B onboarding.
evals.expected_output: The skill should locate and read the existing goal file in the authentication feature folder, apply the three changes (RSA instead of HMAC, add key rotation criterion, priority to critical), and show the user the before/after. Should not rewrite the entire goal — surgical updates only. Master.md should be updated to reflect the priority change.
expectations: The skill attempts to read the existing goal file at Docs/Goals/authentication/add-jwt-authentication.md
The skill modifies the existing file in place rather than creating a new goal
Priority is changed to critical in the YAML frontmatter
A new acceptance criterion about key rotation (90 days) is added
The HMAC-to-RSA change is reflected in constraints or criteria
Existing criteria that are unaffected remain unchanged
The skill shows the user what changed before or after confirming
The goal title retains the [Feature] Task format
Docs/Goals/Master.md was updated to reflect the priority change to critical
Master.md row for the JWT authentication goal shows priority 'critical'

evals.expected_output: The skill should recognize this is extremely vague and ask probing questions to narrow scope. Should NOT write a goal with vague criteria. Should explore the codebase first, then ask targeted questions about which areas are slow, what metrics matter, and what the performance target is.
expectations: The skill does NOT immediately write a goal file with vague criteria like 'app is faster'
The skill asks at least 2 clarifying questions before attempting to write a goal
Questions target specific ambiguities: which part of the app, what metric, what target
The skill attempts to explore the codebase to understand the project type
If a goal is eventually written, the title uses [Feature] Task format
If a goal is eventually written, it is placed in a feature subfolder under Docs/Goals/
If a goal is eventually written, criteria are specific and measurable (not 'app is faster' or 'performance is acceptable')
If a goal is eventually written, Master.md is created or updated

evals.prompt: I need a goal for adding dark mode to our Flutter app. We're using Riverpod 2.x for state and Material 3 theming. The theme setup is in lib/core/theme/app_theme.dart and the settings screen is at lib/features/settings/settings_screen.dart. I want it to: persist the user's choice in SharedPreferences, follow system default initially, have a toggle in the settings screen, and make sure all 12 screens respect the theme. Priority is medium, no dependencies.
evals.expected_output: The skill should recognize this is well-specified, write a goal with [Dark Mode] prefix in Docs/Goals/dark-mode/ folder, and NOT over-interrogate. Should reference the specific file paths provided. Master.md is created or updated.
expectations: The skill recognizes the request is already detailed and does not ask more than 2 clarifying questions
A goal file is written inside a feature subfolder (e.g., Docs/Goals/dark-mode/)
The goal title uses the [Feature] Task format (e.g., # [Dark Mode] Implement Theme Switching)
Priority is set to medium (as specified by the user)
The Context section references lib/core/theme/app_theme.dart
The Context section references lib/features/settings/settings_screen.dart
Criteria cover: persistence in SharedPreferences, system default, settings toggle, and all-screens coverage
Criteria mention Riverpod where relevant for state management
The goal has between 4-7 acceptance criteria
Docs/Goals/Master.md was created or updated with the new goal row

evals.prompt: I need two goals for authentication: first, add email/password login, and second, add Google OAuth as an alternative login method. The OAuth depends on the email login being done first. Both are high priority.
evals.expected_output: Two goal files written in the same authentication/ feature folder. The email login goal has no dependencies. The OAuth goal depends on the email login goal using the relative path format. Master.md is updated with both goals.
expectations: Two separate goal files were created in Docs/Goals/authentication/
Both goal titles use the [Authentication] Feature prefix
The email/password goal has no dependencies (depends_on: [])
The OAuth goal has depends_on referencing the email login goal with relative path (e.g., authentication/add-email-login.md)
Both files have priority: high
Both files have status: pending
The feature folder is shared (both files in authentication/)
Each goal has its own acceptance criteria section with at least 3 criteria
Docs/Goals/Master.md was created or updated with rows for BOTH goals
Master.md shows the dependency relationship for the OAuth goal

evals.prompt: We already have a goal at Docs/Goals/payments/integrate-stripe.md that is still pending. I want to create a new goal for adding PayPal integration to our payments system.
evals.expected_output: The skill should detect the existing payments/integrate-stripe.md goal during duplicate check, inform the user, and create the new PayPal goal in the same payments/ feature folder — NOT as a duplicate of the Stripe goal. Master.md is updated with the new goal alongside the existing Stripe goal.
expectations: The skill scanned Docs/Goals/ recursively and found the existing payments/integrate-stripe.md
The skill informed the user about the existing related goal in the same feature folder
A new goal file was created in Docs/Goals/payments/ (same feature folder)
The new goal title uses [Payments] prefix (e.g., # [Payments] Add PayPal Integration)
The new goal's depends_on or Notes reference the existing Stripe goal where relevant
The new goal was NOT created as a duplicate of the Stripe goal
The filename is different from integrate-stripe.md
Docs/Goals/Master.md was updated with the new PayPal goal row
Master.md contains rows for BOTH the existing Stripe goal and the new PayPal goal

evals.prompt: I have 3 existing goals already: Docs/Goals/auth/add-login.md (pending, high), Docs/Goals/auth/add-oauth.md (in-progress, high), and Docs/Goals/dashboard/build-dashboard.md (completed, medium). Now I want to add a new goal for adding a user profile page. Medium priority, depends on the login goal being done.
evals.expected_output: The skill should create a new goal file for the user profile feature. When updating Master.md, it must either build it from scratch (scanning all 4 goals including the 3 existing ones) or add a row to an existing Master.md. The final Master.md must contain ALL 4 goals with correct statuses, priorities, and dependencies.
expectations: A new goal file was created (e.g., Docs/Goals/user-profile/add-user-profile-page.md or similar)
The goal title uses [Feature] Task format (e.g., # [User Profile] Add User Profile Page)
The goal has priority: medium
The goal has depends_on referencing the login goal (e.g., auth/add-login.md)
Docs/Goals/Master.md exists after the operation
Master.md contains a summary table with counts for pending, in-progress, completed, and total
Master.md contains rows for ALL 4 goals (auth/add-login.md, auth/add-oauth.md, dashboard/build-dashboard.md, and the new profile goal)
Master.md correctly shows: add-login as pending/high, add-oauth as in-progress/high, build-dashboard as completed/medium, and the new goal as pending/medium
Master.md shows the dependency of the new profile goal on auth/add-login.md
Master.md rows are sorted by priority (high goals before medium goals)

evals.prompt: mark the goal at Docs/Goals/auth/add-login.md as completed
evals.expected_output: The skill should update the status in the goal file's YAML frontmatter from pending to completed. Master.md should be updated to reflect the status change and the summary counts should be recalculated.
expectations: The skill reads the existing goal file at Docs/Goals/auth/add-login.md
The skill updates the status field to 'completed' in the YAML frontmatter
The goal file is modified in-place, not recreated
Docs/Goals/Master.md is updated to reflect the status change
The Master.md row for auth/add-login.md shows status 'completed'
Master.md summary counts are recalculated (pending count decreased, completed count increased)
The skill confirms the change to the user

evals.expected_output: The skill should trigger the Dashboard Workflow: scan all goal files under Docs/Goals/ recursively, reconcile with Master.md (or create it if missing), and present the summary + goals table to the user. Should NOT try to create a new goal.
expectations: The skill does NOT attempt to create a new goal file
The skill scans Docs/Goals/ recursively for existing goal files
The skill reads or creates Docs/Goals/Master.md
If Master.md exists, the skill checks for discrepancies between it and actual goal files
The skill presents a summary table with status counts
The skill presents the full goals table with Feature, Goal, Status, Priority, Dependencies, and Path columns
If no goals exist, the skill informs the user and suggests creating one
If discrepancies are found, the skill rebuilds Master.md from goal files as source of truth

evals.prompt: I need to add a search feature to our e-commerce app. It should support full-text search on product names and descriptions, with filters for category, price range, and rating. Use Elasticsearch. High priority.
evals.expected_output: A goal file with [Search] or [Product Search] prefix, YAML frontmatter including both created AND updated dates, acceptance criteria covering full-text search, filters, and Elasticsearch integration. Master.md created/updated.
expectations: A file was written inside a feature subfolder under Docs/Goals/ (e.g., Docs/Goals/search/ or Docs/Goals/product-search/)
The goal title uses the [Feature] Task format
The file contains YAML frontmatter with status: pending
The file contains YAML frontmatter with priority: high
The file contains YAML frontmatter with a 'created' date in YYYY-MM-DD format
The file contains YAML frontmatter with an 'updated' date in YYYY-MM-DD format
The acceptance criteria mention full-text search on product names and descriptions
The acceptance criteria mention category filter
The acceptance criteria mention price range filter
The acceptance criteria mention rating filter
The context or constraints mention Elasticsearch
Docs/Goals/Master.md was created or updated with the new goal row
Master.md contains a row for the search goal with priority 'high'
